title: Chapter 4 - Branches, Merging, and Conflicts
description: Check your understanding of branches and merging.

options.label: They let you work on changes without breaking the main branch
They only exist on GitHub, not locally
They are required to use Git at all

explanations.overview: Branches are separate lines of work that keep the main branch stable.

byOptionId.a: Correct. Branches isolate work so main stays stable.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. Branches do not delete history automatically.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Branches exist locally and can also exist on remotes.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. You can use Git with only one branch, but branches help a lot.

options.label: Creates a new branch and switches to it

explanations.overview: `-c` means create. `switch` changes your current branch.

byOptionId.a: Incorrect. It does not delete branches.
byOptionId.b: Correct. It creates and switches in one command.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Committing is a separate step.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Pushing to a remote is a different command.

questions.prompt: What does it mean to merge a branch?

options.label: Combine commits from one branch into another

explanations.overview: Merging brings changes from one branch into a target branch (like main).

byOptionId.a: Correct. Merge combines work from branches.
byOptionId.b: Incorrect. File renaming is separate from merging.
byOptionId.c: Incorrect. Uploading happens via push and your repo content.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Merging keeps history; it does not erase it.

questions.prompt: When does a merge conflict usually happen?

options.label: When your monitor is too small
When two branches change the same lines in a file

explanations.overview: Conflicts happen when Git cannot automatically combine changes.

byOptionId.a: Not quite. Screen size does not create Git conflicts.
byOptionId.b: Not quite. `git status` shows conflicts but does not cause them.
byOptionId.c: Correct. Same-line changes often cause conflicts.
byOptionId.d: Incorrect. Conflicts are about changes, not GitHub availability.
